target_scopes: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker
Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker
live_context_hint: Use the ACTE block-pinned Mog Coin address, token and native balances, Uniswap V2 router/pair state, transfer-fee parameters, fee receivers, exemptions, ABI, events, and failed or missing probes.
protocol_focus: Audit the deployed Ethereum fee-on-transfer token for reachable extraction through transfers, fee and burn accounting, automatic token-to-ETH swaps, liquidity addition, public recovery functions, and Uniswap V2 pair interactions.
attacker_profile: unprivileged token holder, approved spender, trader, liquidity participant, pair-facing caller, fee-on-transfer integrator, receiver contract, or flash-liquidity user
core_invariants: No unprivileged caller can move the contract's ETH or ERC20 balances to an attacker-controlled receiver.
Every transfer conserves sender value across recipient amount, contract fee tokens, burn allocation, and total-supply reduction.
Automatic swap and liquidity operations cannot overdraw contract tokens or ETH, misroute proceeds, or create attacker-controlled excess value.
The live Uniswap V2 pair balance and reserve state must remain economically consistent with fee-on-transfer execution and liquidity operations.
Allowance, max-transaction, max-wallet, trading, fee-exemption, and authorization branches cannot be bypassed for profitable extraction.
Owner-only configuration is not an exploit unless an unprivileged path bypasses authorization or abuses already-live configuration.
high_value_surfaces: _transferFrom fee, trading-open, max-transaction, max-wallet, and swap trigger branches
takeFee burn, contract-fee accumulation, total-supply reduction, and Transfer-event accounting
swapBack token-to-ETH swap, ETH fee distribution, and addLiquidityETH execution with zero minimum outputs
manualSend() public native-balance forwarding to autoLiquidityReceiver
clearStuckToken(address,uint256) public ERC20 forwarding to autoLiquidityReceiver
showBacking(uint256), showSupply(), pair balance, DEAD balance, and ZERO balance arithmetic
approve(address,uint256), approveMax(address), and transferFrom allowance handling
owner-only setParameters, setStructure, setWallets, setSwapBackSettings, startTrading, reduceFee, maxWalletRule, removeLimits, and ownership changes
impact_mapping: Fund extraction requires attacker-controlled MOG, ETH, LP value, WETH, USDT, USDC, or another token gain at the contract, pair, liquidity provider, or user expense.
Reward extraction requires an unprivileged caller to receive fee or liquidity proceeds beyond a valid live entitlement.
Supply, fee, reserve, allowance, or balance corruption counts only when connected to a reachable transaction producing attacker-controlled value.
Public recovery functions count only if the attacker can redirect the configured receiver or otherwise capture the forwarded assets without privileged compromise.
known_rejection_memory: Reject owner-only fee, wallet, trading, limit, or ownership changes without an unprivileged authorization bypass.
Reject generic fee-on-transfer incompatibility, slippage, MEV, denial of service, event mismatch, or holder loss without a concrete attacker value gain.
Reject claims that do not match the current direct deployment, verified source, captured block, live pair/router, and live balances.
Reject critical claims without a reproducible local test or pinned-fork proof plan.
